name: Buck v. Davis
question: Did the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit impose an unduly burdensome standard of review--requiring extraordinary circumstances--for granting a Certificate of Appealability (COA) for a claim of ineffective assistance of counsel?
description: A case in which the Court held that the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit applied an unduly burdensome standard of review on a Certificate of Appealability (COA) for a claim of ineffective assistance of counsel when it denied the COA for failing to demonstrate extraordinary circumstances.
